content:
- Read progress log plus Ralph active/done task summaries.
- Identified three active failing tasks from current summary: two report tasks in story-14 and one CI vulnerability-scan task in story-15.
- Next: inspect raw task markup and chooser rules for blocker/priority tags, then write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Determined actual repo stack/architecture from the codebase before choosing: Rust workspace (`Cargo.toml`, `crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`) with Docker image publishing via `.github/workflows/master-image.yml` and workflow contract tests in `crates/runner/tests/support/github_workflow_contract.rs`.
- Resolved active candidates from `.ralph/current_tasks.md`: two story-14 report tasks and one story-15 CI/image pipeline task, all with `<passes>false</passes>`. No active failing bug tasks were present, and none of the active candidates exposed `<blocked_by>` or `<priority>` tags.
- Verified story-15 is the strongest continuation of existing implementation work because story-15 tasks `01` and `02` already pass in `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, while `03` is the remaining unblocked task in that pipeline story. Wrote `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/03-task-add-free-vulnerability-scan-to-image-publish-workflow.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next if resuming after context cutoff: start the selected story-15 task with the `tdd` skill, add contract coverage for the scanner/failure policy in the GitHub workflow tests, implement the workflow change, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and both required skill files (`tdd`, `improve-code-boundaries`).
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/03-task-add-free-vulnerability-scan-to-image-publish-workflow.md`.
- Next: inspect the task file and story plan state to determine whether this turn must produce/update a plan and quit or execute the approved plan.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it has no execution marker yet, so this turn must create the plan artifact rather than implement code.
- Checked story-15 plan/task examples to copy the existing plan structure and inspect how plan paths/status are recorded.
- Next: inspect the current workflow tests/code enough to write a concrete TDD execution plan, then create the new plan file and update the task to point at it with an execution marker.

- Loaded the current master-image workflow and the workflow-contract support/tests to gather concrete interfaces for the planning artifact.
- Planning focus now is to choose a free scanner and decide whether scan policy belongs entirely in `GithubWorkflowContract` or needs any image-contract support.
- Next: write the story-15 task-03 plan with explicit TDD slices, boundary cleanup target, and exact files expected to change, then wire the task file to that plan.

- Verified the current workflow/test boundary after task 02: `GithubWorkflowContract` already owns YAML policy assertions and the workflow already uses least-privilege permissions plus explicit publish gating.
- The remaining planning work is to define the scanner integration as an extension of that same contract boundary, likely around a repository-owned scan report path and explicit failure policy.
- Next: finish repository-context reads, then create the task-03 plan file and update the task to reference it with `NOW EXECUTE` before stopping.

- Confirmed enough repo context to author the task-03 execution plan: current workflow, existing CI contract boundary, existing README CI safety section, and story-15 plan conventions.
- Next edits will be limited to the task markdown and a new plan file under the task-specific `_plans` directory so the next turn can execute under the approved TDD slices.
- After those edits I will stop immediately, per the planning-stage instruction.

- Created the task-03 execution plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/03-task-add-free-vulnerability-scan-to-image-publish-workflow_plans/2026-04-19-free-vulnerability-scan-plan.md`.
- Updated the task markdown to point at that plan. The plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so the next turn should read the task, load the `tdd` skill again, and execute the red-green slices rather than replanning.
- Key design choice captured in the plan: build the release image once as a tarball, scan that exact tarball with a free scanner before any push, upload a visible report artifact, then load/push the same scanned artifact.
- Stopping now because the repo workflow explicitly requires quitting immediately after the planning turn reaches `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Read the active task, confirmed it points to the story-15 task-03 plan, and verified the plan ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Loaded the current master-image workflow and the GithubWorkflowContract support module; current publish path still pushes directly from docker/build-push-action without any scan gate.
- Next: add the first RED contract around build archive -> scan -> push ordering, then implement the minimum workflow change to satisfy it before moving to failure-policy/report slices.

- Completed TDD slice 1: added a CI contract for build archive -> scan -> load -> push ordering, confirmed it failed, then changed the workflow to export a docker archive, scan it with Trivy, load it, and push afterward.
- Also removed one implementation-coupled assumption from the existing publish contract so it checks the public publish behavior instead of requiring docker/build-push-action to do the push itself.
- Next: add the RED contract for explicit Trivy failure policy and report visibility (`always()` print/upload of the report), then implement the smallest workflow change to satisfy it.

- Completed TDD slices 2-4: added/greened a contract for explicit Trivy failure policy plus always-on report visibility, then added/greened a README contract line documenting the vulnerability gate.
- Ran the full `cargo test --test ci_contract` suite and all workflow contracts are green. Reviewed the improve-code-boundaries smell files most relevant here; the main cleanup already done was removing the old contract's coupling to inline docker/build-push-action pushing.
- Next: run the required repo lanes in order (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`), fix any first failing lane, then update the task file and Ralph metadata only after everything is green.
